dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8211 IH: Army Security Agency Monument Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Adams (for herself, Mr. Harris of North Carolina, Ms. Ross, Mr. Subramanyam, and Mr. DeSaulnier) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Armed Services, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of the Army to place in Arlington National Cemetery a monument honoring Army Security Agency veterans of World War II, the Korean conflict, the Vietnam Era, and the Cold War era, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Army Security Agency Monument Act.

section.header: Placement of monument honoring veterans of the Army Security Agency who served in the Armed Forces between 1945 and 1976, during World War II, the Korean conflict, the Vietnam era, and the Cold War era

paragraph.text: Subject to the requirements of subsection (c), the Secretary of the Army shall place in Arlington National Cemetery a monument honoring Army Security Agency veterans who served in the Armed Forces between 1945 and 1976, during World War II, the Korean conflict, the Vietnam era, and the Cold War era.

If the Secretary of the Army is unable to identify an appropriate place in Arlington National Cemetery for the monument described in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall place the monument at a location outside the entrance to Arlington National Cemetery.

subsection.text: The monument placed under subsection (a) shall be—

paragraph.text: based on a design approved by the Secretary of the Army and the National Army Security Agency Association;
placed on a non-burial plot; and
limited to 52 inches by 18 inches in base dimensions.

subsection.text: The Secretary of the Army may only place a monument under subsection (a) if the Secretary enters into an agreement with the National Army Security Agency Association under which the Association agrees to pay all costs necessary for preparation of the site for the monument and to construct, install, and maintain the monument, and to such other provisions as the Secretary may require.
In this section, the terms World War II, Korean conflict, and Vietnam era have the meanings given such terms in section 101 of title 38, United States Code.
